vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #741

plan.title: fix(scripts/release.py): bump VERSION literal in run during release flow

narratives.Description: fix(scripts/release.py): bump VERSION literal in run during release flow
narratives.Labels: bug, blocks-release-tag, ci-cd, operational, determinism, test-debt

items.title: Path A — remove the VERSION literal in `run`; resolve dynamically via the resolve_version.py priority chain

narrative.Action: Path A is locked in (per swarm-prep user direction; preferred per #723 symmetry). Remove the hard-coded `VERSION = "X.Y.Z"` literal in `run` (line ~20). Replace with dynamic resolution mirroring `scripts/resolve_version.py`'s priority chain: (1) `$DEFT_RELEASE_VERSION` env (set by `release.py::run_build`), (2) `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` stripped of leading 'v', (3) `0.0.0-dev` fallback. Eliminates the hand-bumped literal entirely; no drift surface remains.
narrative.Acceptance: After `task release -- 0.X.Y` lands on master, `python run --help` (and every command header) prints `0.X.Y` without any explicit literal-bump step. The `VERSION = "..."` literal no longer exists in `run`.

items.title: CI assertion that run's resolved VERSION matches the latest annotated tag on master

narrative.Action: Add a CI step (in `.github/workflows/release.yml` and/or `task ci:local`) that fails fast if `python run --help` (or equivalent invocation that exposes the resolved version) does not match the latest annotated tag on master. Closes the regression loop for future drift regardless of which fix path was taken.
narrative.Acceptance: On master and on release branches, the CI step fails when the resolved version disagrees with the latest tag. Demonstrated by running CI on a synthetic mismatch fixture in tests.

items.title: Regression test in tests/cli/test_release.py asserts the resolved version matches the released version after `task release`

narrative.Action: Extend `tests/cli/test_release.py` with an assertion that, after running the release flow against a synthetic tag, `run --help` (or equivalent) reports the correct version. Test the full priority chain: env override, git-describe fallback, dev fallback when no tags exist.
narrative.Acceptance: Test green. Existing release tests unchanged. Coverage gap closed (the regression that produced the v0.21.0 stale-VERSION incident is now caught at test time).

narrative.Action: Single `### Fixed` line under `[Unreleased]` noting the dynamic-version resolution change in `run` and citing #741 (this issue) and #723 (sibling fix that established the dynamic-resolver pattern).
narrative.Acceptance: CHANGELOG.md `[Unreleased]` updated. PR template checklist item satisfied.

references.title: Issue #741: fix(scripts/release.py): bump VERSION literal in run during release flow
